subtasks.description: Generate SQL query to fetch top 5 publishers by spend in Q4 2024 (Oct-Dec 2024), including spend amount, transaction count, and trend indicators

Analyze top 5 publishers from SQL results to classify at-risk status based on spend patterns, volatility, contract status, and renewal dates. Generate SAM team summary with risk rankings and recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Split into 2 subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid) for a moderate-complexity aggregation query joining spend/transaction data filtered to Q4 2024 with ranking—a single query handles all 5 publishers and metrics in one call. (2) cross_recon (mid) to reconcile the SQL output with risk indicators (volatility, renewal dates, contract health) and classify at-risk status; classification and SAM summary generation are part of reconciliation, not a separate policy step. Both mid-tier because the SQL involves time-windowed aggregation and the risk assessment requires multi-signal analysis, but neither needs frontier-level reasoning.

output.sql: SELECT publisher, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend, COUNT(*) AS transaction_count, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seats, AVG(amount_usd) AS avg_transaction FROM consumption_facts WHERE event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY publisher ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5

reconciled.sql_value: SELECT publisher, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend, COUNT(*) AS transaction_count, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_seats, AVG(amount_usd) AS avg_transaction FROM consumption_facts WHERE event_date >= '2024-10-01' AND event_date <= '2024-12-31' GROUP BY publisher ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5

output.note: No MongoDB operational data or SQL result rows provided; only the SQL query string was supplied. Reconciliation requires actual publisher records from both sources to classify at-risk status.
